summary: Matched and typed several camera-related functions across the core camera module, debug camera controls, and Home-Run stage camera handling. The PR replaced placeholder stubs with concrete implementations for camera mode checks/restoration, introduced a CameraDebugMode struct for cm_80453004, rewrote debug freecam/orbit helpers to use named fields and vector math APIs, and matched a Home-Run function that copies the main camera state into a target camera. Evidence for motivation is limited to the PR body: "match set camera mode functions", "debug camera mode work", and "homerun camera copy match".
what_changed: Implemented Camera_800300F0 to restore cm_80452C68.mode from cm_80453004.last_mode.
Implemented Camera_8003010C, Camera_80030130, Camera_80030154, and Camera_80030178 as bool mode predicates for camera modes 5, 6, 7, and 8 respectively.
Changed camera.h prototypes for Camera_8003010C, Camera_80030130, Camera_80030154, and Camera_80030178 from UNK_RET/int forms to bool-returning no-argument functions.
Changed cm_80453004 from extern UNK_T to extern CameraDebugMode and added a CameraDebugMode forward declaration.

Added struct CameraDebugMode with fields for last_mode, ply_slot, mode 7 player-follow camera offsets/positions/FOV, mode 8 freecam interest/eye positions/FOV, and an uncertain trailing padding field.
In db_2253.c, added dolphin/mtx/mtxvec.h and replaced more anonymous debug camera temporaries with named cstick, vector, pitch, FOV, and distance variables.
Changed fn_80227904 port parameter from s8 to u8 and renamed/commented it as Debug_UpdateCamera in the implementation.

Implemented fn_802279E8, commented as Debug_OrbitAroundInterest, using HSD_CObjGetEyeVector, PSVECDotProduct, PSVECCrossProduct, PSVECNormalize, PSMTXRotAxisRad, PSMTXMUltiVec, PSVECScale, and PSVECSubtract to orbit a camera eye position around an interest point while clamping pitch between 1 and 179 degrees.
Updated freecam rotation, dolly, and position helpers to access cm_80453004.mode8_eye_pos and cm_80453004.mode8_int_pos by typed struct fields instead of pointer arithmetic over cm_80453004.

Matched fn_8021EB10 in grhomerun.c as a function that copies flags, viewport, scissor, projection, near/far planes, eye position, and interest from the main camera returned by Camera_80030A50 into a target camera, then renders it with fog cleared.
Updated grhomerun.h to include baselib camera/fog/gobj headers and give fn_8021EB10 the typed signature void fn_8021EB10(HSD_GObj* target_cam_gobj).
Updated grHomeRun_8021EEB4 prototype from bool grHomeRun_8021EEB4(UNK_PARAMS) to bool grHomeRun_8021EEB4(void).
smart_moves: Introduced CameraDebugMode before deeply rewriting debug camera code, allowing offsets such as mode8_int_pos and mode8_eye_pos to replace brittle casts like (Vec3*) &cm_80453004 + 0x5.
Kept uncertainty explicit in the new struct with a comment on the trailing x4c padding field rather than overclaiming the layout.
Converted simple mode-check helpers to bool-returning functions, making call sites and headers communicate intent better than UNK_RET or int placeholders.
Used existing HSD_CObj and PSVEC/PSMTX helper APIs to express debug camera movement in terms of eye vectors, left/up vectors, FOV, eye distance, and rotation axes.

For Home-Run camera copy, copied the complete relevant CObj state through getters/setters where available, including viewport, scissor, perspective, near/far, eye, and interest, rather than only copying positions.
Moved function signatures in headers alongside implementation matches so future callers can rely on stronger types.
decomp_lessons: When a global is repeatedly accessed by pointer arithmetic at stable Vec3 offsets, defining the backing struct can make additional functions easier to match and review.

Debug camera code benefits from naming controls by behavior: cstick_x/cstick_y, Debug_UpdateFreecamRotation, Debug_UpdateFreecamDolly, Debug_UpdateFreecamPosition, and Debug_OrbitAroundInterest are more searchable than generic var_f names.
Small camera mode helper functions are often simple comparisons against global mode state; matching them can unlock safer boolean prototypes in headers.
Camera orbit implementations may need pitch clamps near the up vector singularities; this PR used 1.0 and 179.0 degree bounds around acosf(dot(forward, up)).
For CObj duplication, preserve render-region state as well as transform/projection state: flags, viewport, scissor, near/far, perspective, eye position, and interest can all matter.
Do not hide layout uncertainty: padding comments such as "padding? not sure if this is correct" are useful breadcrumbs for later struct refinement.
Changing signedness in controller port parameters should be reflected consistently between C file and header; this PR changed fn_80227904 from s8 to u8 in both places.
naming_conventions: Camera debug global type named CameraDebugMode and stored as cm_80453004.
Debug camera mode fields use mode-prefixed names such as mode7_int_offset, mode7_eye_offset, mode7_eye_pos, mode7_int_pos, mode7_fov, mode8_int_pos, mode8_eye_pos, and mode8_fov.
Player slot in CameraDebugMode is named ply_slot.
C-stick normalized input variables are named cstick_x and cstick_y.
Camera vector locals are named by direction or role: forward_vec, up_vec, left_vec, axis, camera_pos, camera_interest.

Matched-but-still-unknown functions retain address names such as fn_80227904 and fn_802279E8, with explanatory comments like Debug_UpdateCamera and Debug_OrbitAroundInterest rather than renaming exported symbols outright.
Home-Run camera copy function retained symbol fn_8021EB10 but gained a descriptive parameter name target_cam_gobj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced manual absolute-value branches for C-stick deadzone checks with fabs_inline comparisons against 0.2f, while retaining commented old branch structure in the diff excerpt.
Used GET_COBJ(camera) instead of manually accessing camera->hsd_obj for HSD_CObj operations.
Replaced raw Vec3 pointer arithmetic on cm_80453004 with typed field accesses to preserve layout while improving readability.
Used degree/radian conversion constants 57.29578f and 0.017453292f in the orbit routine, matching common decomp patterns around acosf and PSMTXRotAxisRad.
Computed freecam translation scale from eye distance and FOV using 0.03f * (2.0f * (eye_dist * tanf(deg_to_rad * FOV * 0.5f))).
Preserved temporary Vec3 pointer locals in fn_80227FE0, with a comment suggesting they may be fake temps needed for matching.
Matched Home-Run camera copy by using HSD_CObj getters/setters plus direct copy of target_cam_cobj->u from main_cam_cobj->u where no setter is shown in the excerpt.
After setting the target CObj current, cleared fog with HSD_FogSet(NULL), called HSD_GObj_80390ED0(target_cam_gobj, 7U), and ended the current camera with HSD_CObjEndCurrent.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The only human text evidence is the short PR description: "match set camera mode functions", "debug camera mode work", and "homerun camera copy match".
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that refine CameraDebugMode padding or confirm the trailing x4c field layout.
Search for call sites of Camera_8003010C, Camera_80030130, Camera_80030154, and Camera_80030178 to verify mode numbers 5 through 8 semantics.
Investigate whether the fn_80227904 deadzone code intentionally sets cstick_x to 0.0f when fabs_inline(cstick_y) < 0.2f, or whether later PRs corrected this to cstick_y.
Look for later symbol renames replacing fn_80227904, fn_802279E8, fn_80227B64, fn_80227CAC, and fn_80227FE0 with descriptive Debug_* names.
Search Home-Run stage camera functions around grHomeRun_8021EC58 and grHomeRun_8021ED74 for additional camera copy or render-pass behavior.
